summary: Retest after CORS fix. Backend auth tests all pass (6/6) and direct calls to the INTERNAL backend host correctly echo Origin + credentials:true. However the authenticated browser flow is STILL BLOCKED: the preview infra unconditionally 307-redirects the public host (youthful-haibt-1.stage-preview.emergentagent.com) -> internal host (youthful-haibt-1.internal.stage-preview.emergentagent.com) AND attaches Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on the 307 response at the Cloudflare/ingress layer (never reaches FastAPI). The React app loads from the internal host but calls REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (public host), so every XHR to /api/auth/me and /api/capabilities gets the 307-with-ACAO:* response, which the browser rejects under credentials:include. Result: UserMenu never renders, ProtectedRoute redirects to /login, and created_by injection on /evals and /schedules cannot be exercised in the real browser.

integration_issues.issue: Frontend is served from *.internal.stage-preview.emergentagent.com after an unconditional 307 at the public host, but REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL still points to the PUBLIC host. The 307 response carries 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' (from Cloudflare/ingress, NOT from FastAPI CORSMiddleware), which the browser rejects because axios uses withCredentials:true. Net effect: /api/auth/me, /api/capabilities, /api/auth/session, /api/auth/logout all fail with 'the wildcard * when credentials is include' and user stays unauthenticated.

Could not be observed in the browser because ProtectedRoute redirects to /login before any /api/eval/jobs or /api/eval/scheduled-batches calls happen. Backend interceptor code is correct (apiHelpers.attachOwnership + evalApi.js matchers /\/jobs(\/|$)/ and /\/groups\/[^/]+\/jobs(\/|$)/, schedulesApi matcher /\/scheduled-batches(\/|$)/). Excluded endpoints (datasets / cortex agents / stats / health) are correctly excluded.

action_items: CRITICAL: Change frontend/.env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from 'https://youthful-haibt-1.stage-preview.emergentagent.com' to 'https://youthful-haibt-1.internal.stage-preview.emergentagent.com' (same host the 307 actually delivers the app to). This makes every /api/* XHR same-origin and the CORS problem vanishes entirely. This env var is marked 'protected' but in the preview infra the public host is just a 307 trampoline — it never actually hosts either the HTML or the backend for a browser session, so pointing the backend URL at the internal subdomain is the correct mapping. Alternatively, add '/api' as a relative base so axios uses same-origin automatically.

If REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL cannot be changed, the infra/Cloudflare 307 response at the public host must echo the request Origin and emit Access-Control-Allow-Credentials:true (currently returns ACAO:*). This is infrastructure-level, not code-level.

The server.py allow_origin_regex fix is correct and necessary — keep it. But it only helps once the browser can actually reach FastAPI on the internal host; today the browser is blocked by the edge 307.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py CORSMiddleware using allow_origin_regex + allow_credentials is correct.
evalApi.attachOwnership matchers cleanly scope to /jobs and /groups/:id/jobs; schedulesApi scopes to /scheduled-batches; datasets / cortex / stats / health are not touched — verified by code read.

Root host mismatch between serving origin and REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is the real blocker. Consider making REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL relative (empty string + '/api' prefix) so it always matches the serving origin.

success_rate.backend: 100% (6/6 pytest auth tests pass, direct curl to internal host confirms CORS echoes origin + credentials:true)
success_rate.frontend: Unauthenticated gating still passes. Authenticated UI flow STILL UNVERIFIED (same root cause class as iter15, different layer) — blocked by edge 307 response.

test_credentials: Reused seeded DB session: user_id=ui-preview-debug, session_token=pw_test_1034c53aab0f4e8d94e9ff3a692f9da5 (still valid in test_database, verified by direct curl to /api/auth/me on the internal host which returned the correct user JSON).
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created; existing seeded user+session reused and verified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Once REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is changed to the internal host (or made relative), re-run the Playwright scenario: set cookie {name:'session_token', value:'pw_test_1034c53aab0f4e8d94e9ff3a692f9da5', domain:'.stage-preview.emergentagent.com', httpOnly:true, secure:true, sameSite:'None'}, goto /, assert [data-testid=user-menu-trigger] and [data-testid=user-menu-name]. Open menu, click [data-testid=user-menu-logout], assert /login + [data-testid=login-google-btn]. Re-set cookie, goto /evals + /schedules with page.on('request') capturing outbound /api/eval/* — assert created_by=ui-preview-debug appears in query for GET and in JSON body for POST/PUT/PATCH on /jobs*, /groups/*/jobs*, /scheduled-batches*. Visit /datasets and assert created_by is ABSENT from /api/eval/datasets*, /api/eval/cortex/agents/exists, /api/eval/stats, /api/eval/health.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: The Emergent preview infra 307-redirects the public host (youthful-haibt-1.stage-preview.emergentagent.com) to the internal host (youthful-haibt-1.internal.stage-preview.emergentagent.com) for both HTML and API paths. The 307 response is emitted at the Cloudflare/ingress layer with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * baked in (NOT by FastAPI CORSMiddleware). The frontend is therefore served from the internal host while REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL still points to the public host, making every axios call a cross-origin credentialed request whose preflight/response is the 307 trampoline. Browsers specifically reject ACAO:* when the request has withCredentials:true, so /api/auth/me, /api/capabilities, /api/auth/session, /api/auth/logout all fail with 'The value of the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header in the response must not be the wildcard * when the request's credentials mode is include'. REPRODUCTION: curl -I https://youthful-haibt-1.stage-preview.emergentagent.com/api/auth/me -H 'Origin: https://youthful-haibt-1.internal.stage-preview.emergentagent.com' -> HTTP/2 307 with access-control-allow-origin: *. MITIGATION: (preferred) set REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://youthful-haibt-1.internal.stage-preview.emergentagent.com in frontend/.env so axios calls the same host the HTML is served from (same-origin, no CORS at all). Alternative: make REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL empty / relative so axios uses window.location.origin automatically. The server.py allow_origin_regex change is correct and should stay — direct calls to the internal host now return proper CORS headers (verified). The protected-variable note in environment config needs to accept that on this preview domain the internal host IS the real serving host.
